title: Dasystephana puberula
taxonomy: Dasystephana puberula is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. intro: Dasystephana puberula, a flowering plant species of the family Asteraceae, is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dasystephana puberula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Dasystephana puberula is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal ailments.
